mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 4.5% in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which compresses equity risk premiums and lifts S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary regime where CPIAUCSL growth decelerates for three consecutive months, equity markets price in lower terminal rates, generating positive forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positive when CPIAUCSL decelerates for three consecutive months in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in real GDP growth below 1.5% annualized signals an impending recession, causing equity markets to discount future cash flows aggressively and driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or neutral when GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5% in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls coupled with declining unemployment indicate a strengthening labor market that supports corporate revenue growth, leading to positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which improves corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns do not correlate positively with month-over-month INDPRO growth (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When real consumer spending accelerates above its 12-month moving average, it signals robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, pushing equity valuations higher over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative when RSAFS crosses above its 12-month moving average in more than 50% of instances.
